Yonwoo Choi

Computer Science Graduate

Email: yhugestar@snu.ac.kr

About Me

I am currently pursuing a M.S in Computer Science at Seoul National University working in the Visual Computing Lab, where I am fortunate to be advised by Hanbyul Joo. I have previously received a B.A in Computer Science from the University of Virginia and have graduated with distinction.

My research interests are in Computer Vision and Machine Learning, especially in 3D reconstruction and Human Pose Estimation. At the moment, I am interested in mixed reality pose estimation, focusing on egocentric 3D interaction recognition. Ultimately, I dream to build an AI system that can behave like humans in new environments and can interact with humans using a broad channel of nonverbal signals (kinesic signals or body languages).

Projects

Stereo Reconstruction

Stereo Camera Reconstruction using DLT, Triangulation with Non-Linear Optimization.

Stereo Reconstruction is an open-source project that focuses on creating a 3D reconstruction from stereo images. By leveraging the power of computer vision and deep learning techniques, the project aims to accurately estimate depth information from a pair of images captured by two cameras with a known relative position.

Age Classifier

Age Detection Model Trained on Pytorch

Age Classifier is an open-source project that focuses on predicting a person’s age based on facial features. Utilizing deep learning and convolutional neural networks (CNN), the project aims to create an accurate and efficient age estimation model that can be applied to various applications.

Panorama Generator

Panorama image generator using multiple images with a single camera.

Panorama Generator is an open-source project aimed at stitching together multiple images to create seamless, high-resolution panoramas. By employing advanced image processing and computer vision techniques, the project can combine a series of overlapping images taken from different viewpoints to produce a single, wide-angle panoramic image.

Papers

Experience

Visual Computing Lab

Graduate Researcher

November 2021 - Present

The Visual Computing Laboratory aims to enable machines and robots to have human-like visual intelligence, conducting research at the intersection of computer vision, machine learning, artificial intelligence, and graphics.

TA for 3D Computer Vision (Spring 2022)

Research Area: 3D Reconstruction and Human Pose Estimation

Education

Seoul National University

M.S Computer Science and Engineering

Class of 2024

In 1946, Seoul National University was established as a pioneering institution, with a mission to drive human knowledge, nurture innovative leaders, and contribute to an enlightened society. As South Korea's flagship university, SNU is dedicated to fostering intellectual curiosity, academic excellence, and global citizenship, ensuring its continued prominence in Asia and around the world.

Coursework: 3D Computer Vision, Advanced Graphics, Advanced Data Mining, Mobile and Ubiquitous Computing

In 1819, Thomas Jefferson founded the University of Virginia and inaugurated a bold experiment – a public university designed to advance human knowledge, educate leaders and cultivate an informed citizenry.
